commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch preserves compatible Codex context-engine `thread_bootstrap` bindings across compaction and rollover, adds native-thread lifecycle diagnostics/config docs, and extends Codex/config regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +966, Tests +1580, Docs +76, Generated 0. Total +2622 across 22 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main clears context-engine-owned Codex bindings after compaction and rotates at the hard 70k startup guard. I did not run a live current-main Codex/Gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can remove the exported native thread ID and add focused diagnostic-stability coverage; maintainers should still review the session lifecycle policy and stack order afterward.

target.title: fix(codex): preserve semantic native threads across compaction
target.body:
## Summary

This is PR #4 in the Codex native-thread stack, after #85978, #86069, and #86094. It turns the diagnostics foundation into the long-term cache/lifecycle fix for compatible context-engine `thread_bootstrap` bindings:

- preserve matching Codex `thread_bootstrap` bindings across successful context-engine-owned compaction;
- copy preserved bindings to successor session files during transcript rollover, then clear the archived/original binding;
- reread the active Codex binding after forced compaction before rebuilding the Codex prompt;
- keep legacy, ownerless, non-bootstrap, mismatch, and app-server rejection paths rotating/clearing as before;
- add `context-engine-compaction-preserved-binding` lifecycle diagnostics and docs explaining the preserved vs invalidated paths.

## Why this is not “raise 70k to 258k/272k”

The default `70000` native reuse guard remains a legacy/stale-binding safety rail. The healthy Codex + LCM path is semantic ownership:

1. Context engine returns `contextProjection.mode = "thread_bootstrap"` with a stable epoch/fingerprint.
2. OpenClaw injects large assembled context once into the Codex native thread.
3. Later turns resume that native thread and send only the current prompt when the engine/policy/projection/tool identity still matches.
4. Compaction or rollover preserves the binding only when that semantic identity remains safe.
5. Actual app-server overflow/rejection still clears the binding before fresh-thread retry.

The important distinction is model capacity vs warm-thread lifecycle policy:

| Value | Meaning | Share of 70k guard |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `70000` | OpenClaw proactive native-thread reuse guard for legacy/stale bindings | 100% |
| `272000` | OpenClaw Codex runtime input cap used for GPT-5.5 forward-compat rows | 70k is 25.7% |
| `400000` | GPT-5.5 native/context window in current OpenClaw catalog/test fixtures | 70k is 17.5% |

With a 30% post-compaction target, even a compacted transcript can land above the guard:

- 30% of 258k = 77.4k
- 30% of 272k = 81.6k

So repeatedly applying a hard 70k warm-thread rotation rule to otherwise compatible Codex native threads makes long-running sessions take the cold path right after compaction. This PR keeps the 70k guard for legacy/non-semantic bindings, but lets semantically-owned `thread_bootstrap` bindings survive compaction/rollover.

## Local bootstrap size scan

I ran a bounded local scan over named bootstrap candidates (`AGENTS.md`, `USER.md`, `MEMORY.md`, `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `BOOTSTRAP.md`) under `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos`, `/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex`, `~/.openclaw`, and `~/.codex`, using `ceil(bytes / 4)` as a conservative token estimate.

| Metric | Result |
| --- | ---: |
| Candidate files | 3,235 |
| Aggregate directories | 2,032 |
| Aggregate p50 | ~637 tokens |
| Aggregate p90 | ~3.8k tokens |
| Aggregate p99 | ~13.5k tokens |
| Max aggregate | ~117.7k tokens (`~/.codex/memories/MEMORY.md`) |
| Largest repeated AGENTS candidate | ~23.6k tokens |

Interpretation: the bug is not “every AGENTS.md is over 70k.” The failure mode is total rendered pressure: compacted history + context-engine projection + workspace bootstrap/developer instructions + native rollout growth. Once that total has created a warm native thread, the efficient Codex path is to preserve the semantically valid native thread rather than reproject the large stable payload every turn.

## Flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A["Long-running Codex session"] --> B{"Saved native binding?"}
  B -- "Legacy / ownerless / non-bootstrap" --> C{"session/native tokens >= guard?"}
  C -- "yes" --> D["Rotate cold: thread/start + reproject context"]
  C -- "no" --> E["Try normal resume"]
  B -- "context-engine thread_bootstrap" --> F{"Engine id + policy + epoch/fingerprint + tools match?"}
  F -- "no" --> D
  F -- "yes" --> G["Resume warm native thread"]
  G --> H["Send current prompt only"]
  H --> I{"Context-engine compaction?"}
  I -- "same file, identity stable" --> J["Preserve binding in place"]
  I -- "successor rollover, identity stable" --> K["Copy binding to successor; clear archived original"]
  I -- "identity changes or app-server rejects" --> D
  J --> G
  K --> G
```

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Compatible Codex context-engine `thread_bootstrap` native bindings are preserved across successful context-engine compaction and successor session-file rollover, while legacy/non-bootstrap and mismatch paths still rotate/clear.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw worktree on the Lexar-backed checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/worktrees/openclaw-codex-semantic-reuse-guard`, branch `codex/semantic-native-thread-preservation`, with the PR patch applied on top of #86094. The proof probe used temp session files and the actual exported `reconcileContextEngineCompactedCodexBinding(...)`, `writeCodexAppServerBinding(...)`, and `readCodexAppServerBinding(...)` code paths from this checkout.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a one-off Node/tsx proof probe, not Vitest/tsgo and not a local test suite. The probe wrote matching `thread_bootstrap` bindings, invoked compaction-binding reconciliation for same-file compaction and successor rollover, then wrote legacy/non-bootstrap bindings to prove unsafe bindings still invalidate. I also verified the patched worktree identity and whitespace state with `pwd`, `git rev-parse --short HEAD`, `git branch --show-current`, `git status --porcelain`, and `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied console output from the real patched worktree proof probe:

```text
[agent/embedded] context-engine-owned Codex app-server compaction preserved native thread-bootstrap binding
[agent/embedded] context-engine-owned Codex app-server compaction preserved native thread-bootstrap binding
[agent/embedded] context-engine-owned Codex app-server compaction invalidated native thread binding
[agent/embedded] context-engine-owned Codex app-server compaction invalidated successor native thread binding
{
  "sameFileThreadBootstrap": {
    "outcome": {
      "invalidated": false,
      "preserved": true,
      "successorInvalidated": false
    },
    "preservedThreadId": "thread-same-file-warm",
    "projectionMode": "thread_bootstrap",
    "projectionEpoch": "epoch-proof-1"
  },
  "successorRolloverThreadBootstrap": {
    "outcome": {
      "invalidated": false,
      "preserved": true,
      "successorInvalidated": false
    },
    "archivedBindingPresent": false,
    "successorThreadId": "thread-rollover-warm",
    "successorSessionFileBasename": "rollover-successor.jsonl",
    "projectionMode": "thread_bootstrap",
    "projectionEpoch": "epoch-proof-1",
    "projectionFingerprint": "projection-fp-proof"
  },
  "legacyNonBootstrapRollover": {
    "outcome": {
      "invalidated": true,
      "preserved": false,
      "successorInvalidated": true
    },
    "archivedBindingPresent": false,
    "successorBindingPresent": false
  }
}
$ pwd
/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/worktrees/openclaw-codex-semantic-reuse-guard
$ git rev-parse --short HEAD
996ebfe6c4
$ git branch --show-current
codex/semantic-native-thread-preservation
$ git status --porcelain
$ git diff --check
```

- Observed result after fix: Same-file compaction returned `preserved: true` and kept `thread-same-file-warm`; successor rollover returned `preserved: true`, cleared the archived/original binding (`archivedBindingPresent: false`), and copied `thread-rollover-warm` with the same `thread_bootstrap` epoch/fingerprint to the successor session file. The legacy/non-bootstrap rollover returned `invalidated: true`, `preserved: false`, and removed both stale original and successor bindings, proving unsafe bindings still rotate/clear. `git status --porcelain` and `git diff --check` produced no output after the amend.
- What was not tested: No full local Vitest/tsgo suite, package build, or live Gateway/Discord/Codex app-server replay was run locally, to avoid the local resource issue called out in AGENTS.md. Remote GitHub Actions remains the validation gate for type, package-boundary, and test execution.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The motivating production-style logs reported repeated `codex app-server native transcript exceeded active token limit; starting a fresh thread` at native token counts above the 70k reuse guard; issue #86023 and this PR body include the budget math and local bootstrap-size scan explaining that failure mode.

## codex/semantic-native-thread-preservation
```

- Observed result after fix: The patched branch is clean after the amend, `git diff --check` produced no output, and the PR now contains the compaction preservation code path plus focused regression coverage for same-file preservation, successor rollover copy/clear, post-rollover semantic resume, projection mismatch rotation, and non-bootstrap invalidation.
- What was not tested: No live Gateway/Codex app-server replay was run locally, and no local Vitest/tsgo suites were run, to avoid the local resource issue called out in AGENTS.md. Remote GitHub Actions remains the validation gate for type, package-boundary, and test execution.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The motivating production-style logs reported repeated `codex app-server native transcript exceeded active token limit; starting a fresh thread` at native token counts above the 70k reuse guard; issue #86023 and this PR body include the budget math and local bootstrap-size scan explaining that failure mode.


## Validation

Local validation intentionally skipped heavy suites per AGENTS.md / user instruction. GitHub Actions is the validation gate.

- `git diff --check`

Not run locally:

- Vitest focused slices
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`

## Stack / Review Notes

This PR is stacked on #86094. The diff to review is the top commit:

- `996ebfe6c4 fix(codex): preserve semantic native threads across compaction`

Refs #86023.
target.author: 100yenadmin

commands.reason: author association CONTRIBUTOR and repository permission none are not allowed

The legacy/non-bootstrap rollover returned `invalidated: true`, `preserved: false`, and removed both stale original and successor bindings, proving unsafe bindings still rotate/clear. `git status --porcelain` and `git diff --check` produced no output after the amend.
- What was not tested: No full local Vitest/tsgo suite, package build, or live Gateway/Discord/Codex app-server replay was run locally, to avoid the local resource issue called out in AGENTS.md. Remote GitHub Actions remains the validation gate for type, package-boundary, and test execution.

## codex/semantic-native-thread-preservation
